name: Hemorrhoids

key_questions: Bright red blood on toilet paper / in bowl — fresh bleeding?
Age >40 and new-onset bleeding — has colorectal cancer been excluded?
Pregnancy status? (Very common precipitant during pregnancy; affects drug selection — avoid topical HC especially in first trimester)
red_flags: Dark blood, melena, or blood mixed with stool → upper GI bleeding, not hemorrhoidal
Weight loss, anemia, change in bowel habits → colorectal cancer until proven otherwise
Age >40 with new-onset rectal bleeding — colonoscopy/workup needed regardless of apparent hemorrhoidal cause
Severe acute pain + inability to sit → thrombosed external hemorrhoid (urgent care within 72h for excision)
ddx: Anal fissure (sharp tearing pain with bowel movement)
Perianal hematoma (thrombosed external hemorrhoid — acutely painful, tense blue-purple lump at anal verge)
Pruritus ani (chronic perianal itch without structural cause)

treatment.notes: Hemorrhoid Grading (directs referral decision): Grade I — internal, do not prolapse, bleeding only; Grade II — prolapse with defecation, reduce spontaneously; Grade III — prolapse with defecation, require manual reduction; Grade IV — permanently prolapsed, cannot be reduced. Grade I–II: conservative management (pharmacist scope). Grade III: refer for rubber band ligation. Grade IV: refer for surgical hemorrhoidectomy.

first_line.name: Topical Hemorrhoidal Preparations
first_line.drugs: Anusol HC (Canada): hydrocortisone 0.5% + zinc sulfate + bismuth subgallate cream/suppository TID-QID × 5–7 days | Anusol Plus: pramoxine + zinc oxide (NO steroid — for pain/itch without inflammation) | Preparation H HC: hydrocortisone 0.5% cream | Proctosedyl (Rx only): hydrocortisone 0.5% + cinchocaine (dibucaine) + framycetin + esculin — requires prescription | Witch hazel pads (Tucks, Preparation H wipes) PRN for soothing relief
first_line.notes: CRITICAL CANADIAN BRAND CLARIFICATION: Anusol HC in Canada = hydrocortisone 0.5% + zinc sulfate + bismuth subgallate (NOT HC 1% + pramoxine — this is the US formulation). Anusol Plus = pramoxine + zinc oxide (no steroid — useful for pain and itch). Proctosedyl = HC 0.5% + cinchocaine + framycetin + esculin (Rx only in Canada). Topical corticosteroid reduces inflammation and itch. Limit HC-containing products to 5–7 days (risk of skin atrophy with longer use). Apply after bowel movements and at bedtime. Witch hazel (astringent): reduces swelling and soothes — safe in pregnancy. PREGNANCY: Avoid topical HC especially in first trimester; prefer witch hazel, sitz baths, and psyllium. ONTARIO MA SCOPE: Topical HC ± local anesthetic is prescribable under OCP MA (Jan 2023); maximum duration typically 7 days.
first_line.preferred_when: Symptomatic internal or external hemorrhoids Grade I–II with itch and inflammation

first_line.name: Stool Softener + Fibre + Osmotic Laxative
first_line.drugs: PEG 3350 (Restoralax, Lax-A-Day) 17 g dissolved in water daily — preferred osmotic | Psyllium (Metamucil) 1 tbsp in water OD–BID — first-line fibre | Docusate sodium (Colace) 100 mg BID — stool softener (limited evidence)
first_line.notes: ADDRESS THE CAUSE. Softer stool reduces straining which causes hemorrhoid formation. PEG 3350 (Restoralax/Lax-A-Day 17 g daily): preferred osmotic laxative — well-tolerated, good evidence for constipation relief. Psyllium fibre: most evidence for reducing hemorrhoid bleeding and symptoms (Cochrane). Docusate sodium: commonly used stool softener; note limited independent evidence for hemorrhoid outcomes (Cochrane 2014 found insufficient evidence vs placebo). Adequate hydration (8+ glasses/day) essential with fibre.
first_line.preferred_when: Constipation contributing to hemorrhoids — use in all patients

second_line.name: Sitz Baths
second_line.drugs: Warm water sitz bath 15–20 min 2–3× daily
second_line.notes: Significant symptomatic relief for external hemorrhoid discomfort. Promotes healing. Can add Epsom salts (magnesium sulfate). Safe in pregnancy.

second_line.name: Micronized Purified Flavonoid Fraction (MPFF)
second_line.drugs: Daflon 500 mg (micronized purified flavonoid fraction): 2 tablets BID with meals × 7–14 days for acute hemorrhoidal attack
second_line.notes: Cochrane review (2018) demonstrates significant reduction in hemorrhoid bleeding, pain, itching, and discharge. Recommended in ASCRS 2018 guideline. Available in Canada as a natural health product. Mechanism: phlebotonic — increases venous tone, reduces capillary permeability. Well-tolerated. Consider for Grade I–II hemorrhoids with persistent bleeding despite conservative management.

non_pharm: High-fibre diet (25–35 g/day) — fruits, vegetables, whole grains
Avoid prolonged sitting on toilet (take phone elsewhere)
Avoid straining — do not hold breath while defecating (bear down gently)
Regular physical activity to promote regular bowel movements
Moist wipes instead of dry toilet paper (gentler cleansing)
Witch hazel pads (Tucks) or Preparation H wipes — astringent, soothing, safe in pregnancy
refer_when: No improvement after 2 weeks of appropriate conservative treatment
Significant or persistent rectal bleeding (rule out colorectal neoplasia)
Thrombosed external hemorrhoid — excision most effective within 72h of onset
Age >40 with first episode of rectal bleeding (rule out colorectal cancer — colonoscopy referral)
Grade III hemorrhoids — refer for rubber band ligation
Grade IV hemorrhoids (permanently prolapsed) — refer for surgical hemorrhoidectomy
Suspected anal warts, anal fissure, anorectal abscess, or rectal prolapse as alternative diagnosis

references: Ontario College of Pharmacists. Minor Ailments: Hemorrhoids. OCP Prescribing Standards, O. Reg. 256/24 (2025).
RxTx (Canadian Pharmacists Association). Hemorrhoids — Therapeutic Choices, 8th ed. (2023).
Maron DJ, Bhutiani N. ACG Clinical Guideline: Management of Hemorrhoids. Am J Gastroenterol. 2023;118(5):811–823.
NICE. Haemorrhoids: Clinical Knowledge Summary (CKS). National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (2023).
